sections.paragraph:
ἀναβάλλω (s. βάλλω; Hom.+ in var. mngs.; LXX; Ath. 13, 2 [for ἐξήνεγκεν Gen 1:12) 2 aor. act. ἀνέβαλον LXX, mid. ἀνεβαλόμην LXX; pf. ptc. ἀναβεβλημένος (‘wearing’ a cloak) act. and mid. ‘put off’ (mid.: Hes., Works 410 one should not ἀναβάλλεσθαι anything ἐς αὔριον; Nicol. Dam.: 90 Fgm. 130, 43 Jac.; Diod. Sic. 14, 100, 1 εἰς ἕτερον καιρὸν ἀνεβάλετο; Syntipas p. 52, 2; Jos., Ant. 4, 288; 14, 28; PCairZen 412, 13); mid. in the only instance in our lit., and as legal t.t. adjourn a trial, etc. (Hdt., Demosth. et al.; PTebt 22, 9 [112 b.c.] ‘refer matters’; Jos., Ant. 14, 177) τινά remand someone, adjourn (his trial) (like ampliare alqm. Cicero, Verr. 1, 29) ἀνεβάλετο αὐτοὺς ὁ Φῆλιξ Ac 24:22.—M-M.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

var. var. = various(ly)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

aor. aor. = aorist

act. act. = active

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Syntipas Syntipas , a collection of fables under the name of Syntapis; before XI a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

PCairZen PCairZen = Zenon Papyri, Catalogue général . . . du Musée du Caire—List 4

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

t.t. t.t. = terminus technicus (termini technici), technical term(s)

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 58
